Package org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise

Class EntitySearchMetadata

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise.EntitySearchMetadata
public class EntitySearchMetadata extends Object

Java class for EntitySearchMetadata complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="EntitySearchMetadata">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="entityName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="errorMetadata" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}EntityErrorMetadata" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="fieldMetadata" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}FieldLevelSearchMetadata" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="intentQueryMetadata" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}EntityIntentQueryMetadata" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="searchPromotionMetadata" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}EntitySearchPromotionMetadata" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="spellCorrectionMetadata" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}EntitySpellCorrectionMetadata" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getFieldMetadata

      public List<FieldLevelSearchMetadata> getFieldMetadata()
      Gets the value of the fieldMetadata property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the fieldMetadata property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getFieldMetadata().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FieldLevelSearchMetadata

      Returns:
      The value of the fieldMetadata property.
